title: Release Theory based on Merchandise
wikitext:
[]: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by Blockio » Sat Mar 03, 2018 12:47 pm First of all, this entire post is speculation and (to my knowledge), nothing has been confirmed by anybody , so take it with more than just a grain of salt. Now on to the actual topic. I dont have any statistics on merch releases, so this is just personal observation, but I have noticed a trend recently (or at least I think I have): The overall amount of Eva merch released seems to be increasing, but there is a significant lack of figures of the Units relative to the rest. There are a lot of other possible explanations for this, but to me it feels like Khara is giving out merchandising licenses more freely to build up some hype, while the modeling companies are holding back on figure releases to save up their ressources for the new Units we will see in FINAL

[]: Re: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by kuribo-04 » Sun Mar 04, 2018 6:51 am I choose to believe this.

[]: Re: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by Cybermat47 » Sun Mar 04, 2018 7:34 am You have a point. I’ve seen way more Eva merchandise in the nearest anime store lately.

[]: Re: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Apr 01, 2018 4:02 pm Is this mirrored in the Japanese market? It could also just be more Western stores wising up to the spending habits of the non-Japanese Eva fandom and increasing stock accordingly.

[]: Re: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by Blockio » Sun Apr 01, 2018 4:42 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Is this mirrored in the Japanese market? It could also just be more Western stores wising up to the spending habits of the non-Japanese Eva fandom and increasing stock accordingly.

[]: Re: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by AdamMalkovitch » Mon May 07, 2018 1:38 pm This isn't normal? I thought having like 10 character figures for every 1 Evangelion Unit model was just a thing, just part of the marketing side of Eva and that side's obsession with the female characters and selling merch of them, the same side of Eva that created all those shitty slice of life spinoffs and dating sims and motherfucking Petit Eva @ School.

[]: Re: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by Ambiant23 » Mon May 07, 2018 4:46 pm AdamMalkovitch wrote: Petit Eva @ School.

[]: Re: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue May 08, 2018 6:40 pm I, for one, love Petit Eva @ School. Though, the first half of the shorts are much better than the last half.

[]: Re: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue May 08, 2018 9:37 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I, for one, love Petit Eva @ School. Though, the first half of the shorts are much better than the last half.

[]: Re: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by bladerj » Tue Jul 17, 2018 7:40 pm Blockio wrote: First of all, this entire post is speculation and (to my knowledge), nothing has been confirmed by anybody , so take it with more than just a grain of salt. Now on to the actual topic. I dont have any statistics on merch releases, so this is just personal observation, but I have noticed a trend recently (or at least I think I have): The overall amount of Eva merch released seems to be increasing, but there is a significant lack of figures of the Units relative to the rest. There are a lot of other possible explanations for this, but to me it feels like Khara is giving out merchandising licenses more freely to build up some hype, while the modeling companies are holding back on figure releases to save up their ressources for the new Units we will see in FINAL

[]: Re: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 19, 2018 3:08 am Bumping this was unnecessary...

[]: Release Theory based on Merchandise Post by Blockio » Sat Mar 03, 2018 12:47 pm First of all, this entire post is speculation and (to my knowledge), nothing has been confirmed by anybody , so take it with more than just a grain of salt. Now on to the actual topic. I dont have any statistics on merch releases, so this is just personal observation, but I have noticed a trend recently (or at least I think I have): The overall amount of Eva merch released seems to be increasing, but there is a significant lack of figures of the Units relative to the rest. There are a lot of other possible explanations for this, but to me it feels like Khara is giving out merchandising licenses more freely to build up some hype, while the modeling companies are holding back on figure releases to save up their ressources for the new Units we will see in FINAL
